originalBehavior: The published route opened directly into the bias-variance story shell without a separate editorial hero, chapter rail, or play-first framing layer.
localStatus: The local route now adds the shared Engineering Sandbox shell with a local font stack, stronger editorial hero, chapter jump navigation, and play-first prompts while leaving the original scroll, LOESS, KNN, and double-descent runtime intact.
notes: The shell is additive and scoped through body data attributes rather than a rewritten layout system.
Chapter links are generated from data-story-chapter markers so the route keeps its original section order and DOM IDs.
evidence: The pilot plan requires a shared Engineering Sandbox layer with local Be Vietnam Pro and IBM Plex Mono assets, a hero, play-first prompt, and chapter jump rail.
Shared smoke coverage now checks that the route exposes the Engineering Sandbox shell before exercising the original decomposition and sandbox scenes.

originalBehavior: The published article advances through underfitting, overfitting, and error-decomposition scenes with a sticky chart shell whose state changes as readers move through the steps.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original step containers, scroll-linked chart shell, and compiled transitions so the narrative still drives the visuals in order.
notes: The local route preserves the published DOM IDs that the compiled D3 runtime expects.
Only metadata, intro attribution, and closing promo copy were localized around the shipped build.
evidence: The implementation plan treats top-plot and bottom error-decomposition synchronization as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage scrolls into the decomposition and complexity scenes and verifies the active labels change with the narrative.

originalBehavior: Mid-article scenes switch from train and test error views into bias, variance, and noise decompositions while the chart labels and bars update together.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original decomposition chart, KaTeX equation blocks, and synchronized state changes from the published bundle.
notes: The equation hydration still uses the published KaTeX helper bundle.
The chart transitions remain bound to the original scroll steps rather than a rewritten local controller.
evidence: The plan explicitly calls the error-decomposition sync and aggregated trend view parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the decomposition labels appear before the complexity-trend view is activated.

originalBehavior: Later sections expose LOESS smoothing controls plus a K-nearest-neighbors decision-region scene that updates as readers change the active hyperparameters.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published LOESS slider and KNN slider, along with their linked fit and prediction visuals, without rewriting the compiled interaction logic.
notes: The original sandbox containers and slider IDs were preserved for the compiled runtime.
The page still ships the published double-descent chart immediately after the sandbox sections.
evidence: The implementation plan marks the LOESS or smoothing controls and any KNN scene as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage changes the LOESS smoothness and KNN K value and verifies that the rendered state updates.

originalBehavior: The published route included MLU branding, creator bylines, analytics, repo references, and promotional cross-links inside the public body.
localStatus: The local route removes those public-shell references, localizes the remaining shell copy, and uses the shared footer as the single live original-page reference surface.
notes: Docs retain the upstream repo and provenance notes.
The public route no longer includes hardcoded upstream share or promo surfaces in the body.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks creator names, MLU branding, upstream domains, analytics, and promo links in shipped route code.
The route now exposes only the shared-footer link back to the live original page.
